evidence.text:
Diagnosis: symptomatic structural focal epilepsy
Previous meningioma resection 3rd January 2005

Seizure type and frequency: focal seizures with altered awareness every 3 weeks
He will get a warning of an unusual burning taste and then lose awareness and contact for a few minutes. His wife said that he will stare and occasionally chew his lips during these events.
Current anti-epileptic medication: lamotrigine 75mg bd (to reduce and stop as detailed below)
Diagnosis: single focal seizure
CVA with right hemiparesis 2017
He developed shaking of the right arm. This continued for three minutes. The movements did not become generalised.
Medication : simvastatin
Medication
I would suggest maybe Levetiracetam 250 mg once-a-day, increasing by 250 mg every two weeks to 250 milligrams twice a day.
I reviewed this 42 year old lady with epilepsy, together with her husband, in clinic today.
I think these are in keeping with temporal lobe onset focal seizures.
previously she was getting events with coloured objects in her field of vision which were probably occipital lobe seizures.
Currently she is taking sodium valproate 500 mg twice a day
Currently she is taking sodium valproate 500 mg twice a day and levetiracetam 1000 mg twice today
At the same time, she should introduce lamotrigine at 25 mg every day, increasing by 25 mg every fortnight, to a target dose of 75 mg twice a day.
Diagnosis: generalised tonic clonic seizures with myoclonic jerks, possible JME

topiramate 100 mg twice a day

The history is suggestive of juvenile myoclonic epilepsy given that she had an EEG with spike, polyspike and wave activity.

Diagnosis: myoclonic jerks since teenage years

Diagnosis: Focal epilepsy
The smaller episodes are focal seizures with altered awareness
The episodes during sleep are focal to bilateral convulsive seizures
Diagnosis: probable juvenile myoclonic epilepsy
She gets generalised seizures once a week.
The absences and jerks happen more frequently perhaps several times a day.
She is currently taking clobazam 10 mg od
Sodium Valproate 200 mg twice a day (to be increased to 300 mg BD in steps of 100 mg every two weeks)
Diagnosis: probable juvenile myoclonic epilepsy
Lisa continues to get jerks several times a day.
She also gets tonic clonic seizures.
Medication: Topiramate 100 mg twice a day
Epilim 400 mg twice a day
Diagnosis: juvenile myoclonic epilepsy
generalised tonic clonic seizures, 1 since previous appointment
Myoclonic jerks weekly
Occasional absences.
lamotrigine 100 mg BD

Diagnosis: temporal lobe epilepsy

Thyroxine

decided to switch the carbamazepine to eslicarbazine
Zonisamide 100mg bd
Looking through her old notes it seems that levetiracetam gave her mood disorder
lamotrigine wasn’t effective
Diagnosis: juvenile myoclonic epilepsy
She started noticing that her arms were “jerky” in the morning when she was 13 years old.
She has suffered from depression in the past but her mood has been stable for years after starting the citalopram
Diagnosis 1. Symptomatic structural epilepsy secondary probably caused by early life meningitis
